rows.source_job: feel reassured the design is defensible
rows.stmt: Minimize the number of gaps in the recommended criteria that a reviewer could challenge as unsupported
rows.imp_rat: Minimizing challengeable gaps in the criteria directly serves the engineer's need to present a defensible, review-proof design package.
rows.sat_rat: Design-review checklists catch some gaps, but the status quo offers no systematic way to prove the criteria set is complete before review.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC Design Engineer (A&E / Process Architect)
rows.source_job: feel reassured the design is defensible
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of worry that the recommended conditioning criteria will fail under real-world operating demand
rows.imp_rat: Worry that criteria will fail under real-world operating demand reflects the engineer's need to feel the design is robust, not just compliant on paper.
rows.sat_rat: Safety margins and standards reduce this worry, but real operating loads such as gowning, occupancy and equipment heat remain hard to fully anticipate.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of underspecified commercial terms when translating classified-zone airflow needs into the sourcing specification
rows.imp_rat: Underspecified commercial terms when translating classified-zone needs create downstream disputes this lead must own.
rows.sat_rat: Spec templates help, but nuanced GMP airflow requirements are still translated imperfectly into commercial terms.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of uncertainty in the cost-benefit figures presented for capital approval
rows.imp_rat: Uncertain cost-benefit figures jeopardize the capital approval this lead must secure to proceed with the acquisition.
rows.sat_rat: Finance business-case templates exist but airflow benefit quantification remains soft, so uncertainty persists at approval.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of dispute over whether contracted classified-zone performance levels have been met
rows.imp_rat: Disputes over whether contracted classified-zone performance was met expose this lead to unresolved liability and rework.
rows.sat_rat: Acceptance testing helps adjudicate performance, but ambiguous metrics still leave room for dispute in current contracts.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of suppliers misinterpreting the solicitation scope for classified-zone capability
rows.imp_rat: Supplier misinterpretation of solicitation scope produces mispriced, non-comparable bids that undermine the competitive-offer step.
rows.sat_rat: Detailed RFQ packages reduce misreads, but technical scope for classified zones is still frequently interpreted inconsistently.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of unease that the award rationale could be challenged and not hold up under scrutiny
rows.imp_rat: Unease that the award rationale could fail under challenge reflects the accountability this sourcing lead carries.
rows.sat_rat: Structured evaluation records support defensibility, but comparability weaknesses leave rationale partly vulnerable.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of feeling exposed by a gap in the documented basis for the award
rows.imp_rat: Feeling exposed by a gap in the documented award basis matters to a lead whose decisions face audit and leadership scrutiny.
rows.sat_rat: Procurement documentation practices give reasonable coverage, but technical justification gaps still create exposure.

rows.source_job: The Trusted Steward of Secure Supply
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a supply continuity or compliance failure eroding standing as steward of secure classified-zone supply
rows.imp_rat: A supply or compliance failure eroding standing as steward of secure supply is the reputational stake tied to this role's identity.
rows.sat_rat: Current controls reduce catastrophic failure odds, but residual continuity risk keeps this standing partially exposed.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variability in how airflow conformance is verified across classified zones over time
rows.imp_rat: Variability in how conformance is verified over time weakens the defensibility of the ongoing-compliance evidence the QA manager certifies.
rows.sat_rat: Scheduled monitoring routines provide some consistency, but method and personnel drift over years still introduces variability the status quo does not fully control.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether classified zones are currently inside their validated cleanliness state
rows.imp_rat: Reducing uncertainty about whether zones are currently in-state gives the QA manager the confidence to certify ongoing operations without constant doubt.
rows.sat_rat: Live dashboards provide reassurance for monitored parameters, but coverage gaps mean the manager cannot be fully certain of every zone at every moment.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of last-minute scrambling to assemble airflow cleanliness evidence ahead of an inspection
rows.imp_rat: Last-minute scrambling before inspections signals weak steady-state readiness and drains QA resources, though it does not itself risk the validated state.
rows.sat_rat: Pre-inspection evidence assembly is still a widespread fire-drill in most sites, indicating the status quo poorly supports continuous readiness.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of being caught unaware by a lapse in the validated state between requalification cycles
rows.imp_rat: Being caught unaware by a lapse would blindside me publicly, so avoiding surprise about the validated state matters emotionally and reputationally.
rows.sat_rat: Interval-based checks mean lapses can accrue silently, so the current state offers limited early warning.

rows.source_job: feel confident the recommendation will hold
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of being blindsided by a challenge to the evidence behind the recommendation
rows.imp_rat: Being blindsided by a challenge to the evidence threatens the specialist's professional confidence and defensibility in front of regulators.
rows.sat_rat: Peer review and QA scrutiny catch some weaknesses, but surprise challenges during inspections still occur.

rows.source_job: feel reassured the project won't derail
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of feeling blindsided by a supplier or scope event once the capital is locked
rows.imp_rat: Feeling blindsided by a supplier or scope event after capital lock reflects real exposure this steward wants to preempt.
rows.sat_rat: Post-lock events still catch teams unaware despite contracts and risk registers.

rows.stmt: Minimize the number of gaps in the operating record that fail to demonstrate conditions were maintained through the cycle
rows.imp_rat: Gaps in the operating record undermine the GMP evidence that conditions held, which is part of this operator's documentation duty.
rows.sat_rat: Continuous electronic monitoring auto-logs most parameters, though manual entries and sensor dropouts still create occasional gaps.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood that captured operating evidence cannot be trusted as a faithful account of actual zone conditions
rows.imp_rat: Untrustworthy operating evidence jeopardizes the GMP record the operator must produce to prove conditions held.
rows.sat_rat: Validated data-integrity controls and calibrated sensors make records generally trustworthy, though sensor drift can still distort them.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood that scheduled maintenance leaves the conditioning capability outside its qualified operating state
rows.imp_rat: If maintenance leaves the system outside its qualified state, the operator inherits a non-compliant zone, so requalification-clean maintenance matters directly to the job.
rows.sat_rat: Post-maintenance checks and requalification steps usually restore the qualified state, but verification depth varies by site procedure.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variation in particulate removal performance across the air supply over time
rows.imp_rat: Consistent particulate removal over time protects the classified environment the operator sustains, though degradation is gradual.
rows.sat_rat: Filter loading is tracked via DP and periodic integrity tests, giving reasonable but not continuous visibility of removal variation.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of doubt that the classified zones stayed within target ranges through the entire production cycle
rows.imp_rat: Confidence that zones held through the cycle is the emotional payoff of doing this operator's job well and reduces end-of-batch stress.
rows.sat_rat: Continuous trend records give reasonable reassurance, but residual doubt about unmonitored moments persists.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of parameter breaches that would undermine leadership's trust in this operator to keep zones within qualified parameters
rows.imp_rat: Repeated parameter breaches erode leadership's trust in the operator's ability to keep zones qualified, a real status concern.
rows.sat_rat: Current control keeps breaches relatively infrequent, so trust is generally maintained though not fully assured.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of a setpoint being held inconsistently across changing external load conditions
rows.imp_rat: Holding a setpoint consistently across changing external loads is essential to steady classified-zone conditions through seasonal and occupancy swings.
rows.sat_rat: Feedback control compensates for most load variation, but extreme external swings still cause some setpoint inconsistency.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to stabilize zone conditions after an operational transition
rows.imp_rat: Faster stabilization after an operational transition shortens the window when a classified zone sits outside tight tolerance.
rows.sat_rat: Tuned loops re-stabilize transitions reasonably quickly, though large mode changes still involve extended settling times.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of condition swing during the recovery period following a transition
rows.imp_rat: Condition swing during recovery after a transition risks breaching classified-zone limits, which directly threatens the technician's job of holding sterile-zone conditions.
rows.sat_rat: Modern BMS PID loops damp recovery reasonably well, but overshoot during aggressive transitions is still routinely observed in the status quo.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of a tuned sequence introducing oscillation or hunting in zone conditions
rows.imp_rat: When the technician tunes a sequence, introduced oscillation or hunting destabilizes the very zone conditions they are trying to hold steady.
rows.sat_rat: Standard PID tuning practice usually avoids sustained hunting, but iterative manual tuning still occasionally leaves loops oscillating.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC Design Engineer (A&E / Process Architect)
rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of drift in temperature and humidity setpoints away from the values required to sustain each classified zone's state
rows.imp_rat: Specifying setpoints that resist drift is core to sustaining each classified zone's environmental state, though drift is largely a controls/operational outcome downstream of the spec.
rows.sat_rat: Standard setpoint bands and control loops handle steady-state well, but designers have limited visibility into real drift until the system runs.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC Design Engineer (A&E / Process Architect)
rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of specifying setpoints whose allowable tolerance bands are left undefined for a classified zone
rows.imp_rat: Leaving tolerance bands undefined makes setpoints unqualifiable and unmonitorable, so defining them is integral to the setpoint-specification task.
rows.sat_rat: Standard practice attaches tolerance bands to critical parameters, though bands for secondary setpoints are sometimes omitted in early specs.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC Design Engineer (A&E / Process Architect)
rows.stmt: Prevent the number of setpoints specified that conflict with the conditioning needs of an adjacent classified zone
rows.imp_rat: Setpoints conflicting with an adjacent classified zone can undermine the whole cascade, so coherence across zones is an important part of defining setpoints.
rows.sat_rat: Integrated zoning schedules usually reconcile adjacent setpoints, though conflicts still surface at interfaces in complex facilities.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC Design Engineer (A&E / Process Architect)
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of error in the load assumptions used to match capacity to each zone's conditioning demand
rows.imp_rat: Accurate load assumptions underpin matching capacity to conditioning demand, so error here propagates into all downstream capacity decisions.
rows.sat_rat: Standard load-estimation methods are mature, but assumptions about occupancy, equipment gains and infiltration in fill-finish rooms still carry real uncertainty.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC Design Engineer (A&E / Process Architect)
rows.source_job: Authority on Compliant Zone Air Design
rows.stmt: Reduce the number of audit findings traced to recommended criteria that undermine the perception of being the definitive authority
rows.imp_rat: Audit findings traced back to recommended criteria directly undermine the engineer's perceived status as the definitive authority on compliant zone air design.
rows.sat_rat: Documented, standards-based criteria limit attributable findings, but interpretive gaps can still trigger audit observations against the designer.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in the monitoring frequency and limits specified for verifying airborne control
rows.imp_rat: Ambiguous monitoring frequency and limits weaken verification of airborne control that the microbiologist specifies.
rows.sat_rat: Established EM programs and compendial guidance provide fairly clear frequency/limit conventions, leaving modest residual ambiguity.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of interpretive ambiguity in airflow conditioning data presented to oversight bodies
rows.imp_rat: Unambiguous data presentation to oversight protects the manager from misinterpretation that could escalate into findings.
rows.sat_rat: Trend graphs and reports exist but frequently require expert interpretation, leaving room for ambiguity in front of auditors.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of doubt that the airflow conditioning data will withstand scrutiny at any moment of inspection
rows.imp_rat: Confidence that data will withstand inspection scrutiny at any moment is central to facing oversight without dread.
rows.sat_rat: Validated systems bolster confidence, but doubts about completeness and defensibility linger given manual reconciliation needs.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of being caught unprepared by an oversight question about classified-zone airflow control
rows.imp_rat: Being caught unprepared on an airflow question threatens the manager's credibility before oversight, an important emotional stake.
rows.sat_rat: Inspection readiness preparation helps, but the breadth of possible questions leaves residual exposure under current tools.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of discovering airflow conditioning data gaps only at the moment oversight asks for proof
rows.imp_rat: Discovering data gaps only when oversight asks is a dreaded surprise this manager wants to prevent proactively.
rows.sat_rat: Periodic self-audits catch some gaps in advance, but no continuous gap-detection means late discoveries still happen.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether current readings truly reflect a stable classified-zone state
rows.imp_rat: Certainty that current readings truly reflect a stable zone underpins the manager's peace of mind about real conditions.
rows.sat_rat: Calibrated sensors give reasonable trust, but sensor drift and placement limits leave some uncertainty about actual zone state.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of protocol revisions caused by gaps discovered after the protocol is approved
rows.imp_rat: Post-approval protocol revisions signal upstream gaps and delay execution, eroding schedule and credibility though not directly compromising the zone.
rows.sat_rat: Template maturity and prior-protocol reuse reduce late revisions, yet gaps still surface after approval in complex conditioning protocols.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of an invalid test result that must be discarded and repeated during verification
rows.imp_rat: Discarded invalid results waste scarce testing windows and delay release, though they mainly cost time rather than jeopardizing the final verdict.
rows.sat_rat: Calibrated instruments and pre-test checks reduce invalid runs, but airflow test setup errors and environmental variability still force repeats.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of unease about hidden gaps in the evidence the verdict rests upon
rows.imp_rat: Unease about hidden evidence gaps directly undermines my confidence in the verdict I must defend.
rows.sat_rat: Structured reviews reduce but never fully dispel the sense that an overlooked gap could exist in the evidence set.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time to align the contamination-control rationale with updated aseptic regulatory expectations
rows.imp_rat: Time to realign rationale with updated aseptic expectations matters given Annex 1 evolution, but is a change-management concern rather than core risk.
rows.sat_rat: Regulatory intelligence exists but re-justifying airflow rationale against new expectations remains a lengthy exercise.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of revisions to the financial case after the funding committee raises challenges to its assumptions
rows.imp_rat: Rework of the financial case after committee challenges wastes cycle time but is a recoverable, procedural setback for this steward.
rows.sat_rat: Standard business-case templates reduce but do not eliminate challenge-driven revisions.

rows.source_job: feel confident the case will hold up
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of doubt about whether the financial assumptions will survive funding committee scrutiny
rows.imp_rat: Doubt about assumptions surviving committee scrutiny undermines the steward's confidence in presenting the case.
rows.sat_rat: Structured business-case methods build some assurance, but assumption fragility persists.

rows.source_job: feel confident the case will hold up
rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of being caught off guard by a gap in the justification during the funding review
rows.imp_rat: Being caught off guard on a justification gap during funding review damages the steward's credibility and stalls approval.
rows.sat_rat: Pre-review vetting reduces surprises somewhat, but blind spots in assumptions still emerge in committee.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of iterative re-adjustment cycles needed to bring every zone to its intended airflow share
rows.imp_rat: Fewer re-adjustment cycles saves the operator time and reduces zone disturbance during balancing, a moderate efficiency stake.
rows.sat_rat: Manual iterative balancing typically needs several passes to converge, so this is poorly served by current methods.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency with which a balanced zone drifts out of its airflow share after an adjacent damper is adjusted
rows.imp_rat: Balancing interaction where one damper adjustment upsets a settled zone is a real nuisance that forces the operator to rework the cascade.
rows.sat_rat: Manual balancing lacks coupled control, so adjusting one damper routinely disturbs neighboring zones under status-quo methods.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC Design Engineer (A&E / Process Architect)
rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of reworking classification criteria after a regulatory or grade requirement is reinterpreted
rows.imp_rat: Reworking classification criteria after regulatory reinterpretation costs the designer effort, but adapting to guidance shifts is an accepted part of the job.
rows.sat_rat: The Annex 1 revision cycle repeatedly forces criteria rework, and current documents are not structured to absorb reinterpretations gracefully.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of ambiguity in the airflow cleanliness acceptance criteria applied to each classified zone
rows.imp_rat: Ambiguous acceptance criteria create interpretation risk during verification and inspection, which the QA manager must minimize to defend conformance decisions.
rows.sat_rat: Standardized grade tables reduce ambiguity for most parameters, though edge cases like recovery and at-rest vs in-operation states still cause confusion.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to retrieve airflow cleanliness records when requested by an inspector
rows.imp_rat: Quick retrieval of airflow records during an inspection affects the impression of control, though it is a readiness convenience rather than a core sterility risk.
rows.sat_rat: Electronic archives speed retrieval versus paper, but scattered record locations still cause delays when an inspector asks for specific airflow evidence.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent without timely reassurance that classified zones are still holding their validated cleanliness state
rows.imp_rat: Timely reassurance that zones are holding state reduces the ongoing worry the guardian role carries between formal verifications.
rows.sat_rat: Continuous monitoring offers frequent reassurance, but non-continuous parameters leave interval gaps where confidence lapses.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of my qualification judgments being questioned or overturned by quality leadership or regulators
rows.imp_rat: Having my qualification judgments overturned by QA leadership or regulators erodes the authority my role depends on.
rows.sat_rat: Well-supported verdicts are usually upheld, though judgment-based calls on change impact are occasionally challenged.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of documentation rework caused by inconsistencies discovered during evidence review
rows.imp_rat: Documentation rework consumes qualification bandwidth and delays approvals, but it is a cost/efficiency burden rather than a direct compliance risk.
rows.sat_rat: Review-driven rework is endemic because inconsistencies are typically found late in QA review rather than prevented at authoring.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of anxiety that the qualified conditioning state will quietly drift before the next requalification
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety over quiet drift reflects a real emotional load, but it is a byproduct of the technical risk rather than a job outcome I am measured on.
rows.sat_rat: Without dense continuous monitoring, the status quo leaves engineers uneasy about undetected drift between requalifications.

rows.source_job: feel reassured the project won't derail
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of anxiety that an unforeseen cost or schedule shock will emerge after capital is committed
rows.imp_rat: Anxiety over post-commitment cost or schedule shocks reflects the genuine unpredictability this steward carries but is a secondary emotional layer.
rows.sat_rat: Contingency and risk management ease but do not remove the underlying worry about shocks.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of production downtime consumed by performing scheduled maintenance
rows.imp_rat: Maintenance downtime concerns the operator's scheduling but is more a production/efficiency metric than a core conditioning-risk outcome.
rows.sat_rat: Planned maintenance windows reduce disruption, yet cleanroom recovery and requalification still consume substantial downtime.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of repeat maintenance interventions caused by an incomplete first service
rows.imp_rat: Repeat interventions from incomplete first service waste the operator's time and re-expose the zone, a moderate concern.
rows.sat_rat: Maintenance checklists reduce incomplete service, but callbacks still occur when verification is superficial.

rows.source_job: feel in control of the zones
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether emerging drift can still be caught in time on the current shift
rows.imp_rat: Uncertainty about whether emerging drift can be caught in time creates on-shift anxiety that affects the operator's felt control.
rows.sat_rat: Trend visualization helps gauge trajectory, but predicting whether a drift is catchable in time is still largely judgment.

rows.stmt: Reduce the rate of false degradation indications that obscure genuine emerging faults on the air handling unit
rows.imp_rat: False degradation alarms cause alarm fatigue that can bury a genuine emerging AHU fault the technician needs to catch.
rows.sat_rat: Basic alarm filtering exists, but nuisance and false degradation indications remain common in the status quo.

rows.stmt: Reduce the rate at which a previously tuned sequence loses correct response as operating conditions shift
rows.imp_rat: Tuning drift as operating conditions shift gradually degrades control quality, requiring the technician to keep re-intervening to hold conditions.
rows.sat_rat: Fixed-gain loops lose fidelity as seasons and loads change, and adaptive tuning is uncommon in typical AHU BMS today.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time to confirm whether a monitored reading reflects a real condition change or a measurement artifact
rows.imp_rat: Quickly confirming whether a reading is real or an artifact prevents the technician from wasting time or reacting wrongly to a false signal.
rows.sat_rat: Confirming a reading usually means manual checks or waiting for corroboration, which is slow without redundant sensing.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of a recovered condition quietly re-excursioning after the operator believed it was resolved
rows.imp_rat: A quiet re-excursion after a believed-resolved event can silently endanger product, making this more than a purely emotional concern.
rows.sat_rat: Continued alarming would catch a re-excursion, but the status quo offers little proactive confirmation that a fix is durable.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time required to determine the requalification scope triggered by a proposed change to conditioning equipment
rows.imp_rat: Faster requalification-scope determination speeds change closure, but it is a productivity gain more than a compliance-critical outcome.
rows.sat_rat: Scope decisions are largely manual and case-by-case, so turnaround varies with the engineer's experience and available precedents.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time needed to assemble defensible evidence for a contamination-control recommendation
rows.imp_rat: Time to assemble defensible evidence affects the specialist's productivity but is secondary to the correctness of the contamination judgment.
rows.sat_rat: Data systems speed compilation somewhat, but stitching airflow, EM, and risk evidence remains a slow manual effort.

rows.source_job: feel confident the recommendation will hold
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of lingering doubt that the contamination-control recommendation will withstand expert scrutiny
rows.imp_rat: Lingering doubt that the recommendation will withstand expert scrutiny reflects the emotional stakes of standing behind airflow judgments.
rows.sat_rat: Strong data reduces doubt, but the inherent uncertainty of viable contamination keeps residual doubt present.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of moments of uncertainty about whether the airflow scheme is currently keeping contaminants out of classified zones
rows.imp_rat: Recurring uncertainty about whether the airflow scheme is currently excluding contaminants is a real emotional burden for the guardian role.
rows.sat_rat: Real-time physical monitoring eases moment-to-moment uncertainty, yet viable status is never known in real time.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time spent without timely assurance that ingress protection is holding in the classified zones
rows.imp_rat: Time without timely assurance that ingress protection holds drives the specialist's emotional need for continuous reassurance.
rows.sat_rat: Continuous DP and particle monitoring give some ongoing assurance, but viable protection confirmation always lags behind.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of requirement revisions after suppliers have been engaged on the airflow capability sourcing
rows.imp_rat: Late requirement revisions after supplier engagement erode leverage and pricing, harming the commercial-requirement job.
rows.sat_rat: Change control is in place but engineering-driven revisions after RFQ release remain common in pharma projects.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time spent reconciling legal and technical terms before contract execution
rows.imp_rat: Time spent reconciling legal and technical terms delays execution but is a process efficiency concern rather than a core-risk outcome.
rows.sat_rat: Legal-technical alignment is routinely achievable but iterative back-and-forth on classified-zone clauses still consumes cycle time.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variability in how supplier capability is assessed across the supply base
rows.imp_rat: Inconsistent capability assessment across suppliers weakens the reliability of qualification decisions this lead owns.
rows.sat_rat: Standardized supplier scorecards exist but application varies, leaving moderate assessment variability.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time required to confirm a supplier's capability to meet the classified-zone requirements
rows.imp_rat: Speed to confirm supplier capability affects schedule but is secondary to getting the qualification correct for this lead.
rows.sat_rat: Prequalification processes are established but capability confirmation for GMP airflow can still be slow and document-heavy.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of incomplete or non-responsive offers requiring re-solicitation
rows.imp_rat: Non-responsive offers requiring re-solicitation waste cycle time in the competitive-offer step but rarely threaten compliance.
rows.sat_rat: Clear submission requirements reduce re-solicitation, though incomplete GMP-scope responses still occur.

rows.source_job: The Trusted Steward of Secure Supply
rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of being seen to have secured capability on unfavorable total-cost-of-ownership terms
rows.imp_rat: Being seen to have secured capability on poor TCO terms threatens this lead's category-value reputation but is a status concern.
rows.sat_rat: Savings reporting exists, but unquantified lifecycle drivers mean unfavorable TCO can go unrecognized either way.

rows.source_job: The Trusted Steward of Secure Supply
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree to which leadership questions the soundness of sourcing decisions attributed to this role
rows.imp_rat: Leadership questioning sourcing soundness affects this lead's influence but is a downstream consequence rather than the primary job.
rows.sat_rat: Documented rationale generally withstands review, though soft TCO figures can still invite questions.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty in identifying which airflow conditioning tasks most warrant resource investment
rows.imp_rat: Knowing which tasks most warrant investment sharpens resource allocation, a real but supporting element of the job.
rows.sat_rat: Prioritization is largely judgment-based today with little quantified reliability-return data to guide it.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of maintenance performed earlier or more often than airflow conditioning reliability requires
rows.imp_rat: Avoiding premature maintenance conserves budget and reduces intervention risk, a meaningful but not compliance-critical concern.
rows.sat_rat: Predominant calendar-based PM regimes drive over-maintenance because condition-based scheduling is not yet widely adopted for AHUs.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of perceived inconsistency in my verdicts that erodes trust in my authority over conditioning qualification
rows.imp_rat: Perceived inconsistency in my verdicts slowly erodes trust, but it is a softer status concern than an outright compliance failure.
rows.sat_rat: Standardized criteria and protocols promote consistency, though subjective assessments can still appear variable across cases.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of revisions to defined contamination criteria after evaluation has begun
rows.imp_rat: Revising contamination criteria mid-evaluation creates rework and inconsistency, a moderate concern for the specialist's rigor.
rows.sat_rat: Change-control discipline limits mid-stream revisions, though evolving expectations occasionally force them.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of exposure to single-supplier dependency over the project lifecycle
rows.imp_rat: Single-supplier lock-in on a validated AHU raises long-term risk, but qualification constraints often make it a tolerable trade-off for this steward.
rows.sat_rat: Multi-vendor sourcing and approved-vendor lists partially mitigate dependency, though qualified sterile-grade suppliers are limited.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of energy consumed beyond what is required to hold airflow conditioning at target in classified zones
rows.imp_rat: Energy waste is a real cost the manager owns but is subordinate to compliance and reliability for classified zones.
rows.sat_rat: Conservative single-pass, high-air-change designs prioritize sterility over efficiency, so substantial excess energy is spent by default.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time airflow conditioning resources sit idle or misdirected before being applied where they are needed
rows.imp_rat: Efficient direction of conditioning capacity matters for resource stewardship but is secondary to compliance in a classified-zone context.
rows.sat_rat: Many pharma AHUs still run constant-volume oversupply for safety margin, so misdirected/idle capacity is common despite emerging demand-based control.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time required to bring the classified zones to their qualified operating state before production begins
rows.imp_rat: Startup time to qualified state affects production readiness but is a bounded, predictable task rather than a continuous risk for the operator.
rows.sat_rat: Known recovery-time data and staged startup sequences make bringing zones online fairly predictable today.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time to apply a parameter adjustment when zone demand shifts during a production transition
rows.imp_rat: Applying a parameter adjustment promptly when demand shifts helps hold conditions through a transition, though transitions are relatively planned events.
rows.sat_rat: Recipe-driven setpoint changes apply quickly in modern BMS, but manual reparameterization during unplanned shifts remains slower.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of overshoot in temperature, humidity, or pressure following an operating parameter change
rows.imp_rat: Overshoot after a parameter change can briefly push a zone past limits, though it is usually transient and self-correcting.
rows.sat_rat: Ramped setpoint changes and tuned loops limit overshoot, but aggressive changes still produce transient excursions.

rows.stmt: Minimize the likelihood of entering an incorrect parameter value due to an unclear demand state during adjustment
rows.imp_rat: Entering a wrong parameter due to an unclear demand state can push a zone off setpoint, but such errors are typically caught before impact.
rows.sat_rat: Structured HMI entry with limits and confirmations reduces bad entries, though ambiguous demand states still cause occasional mistakes.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of having to reconstruct operating evidence after a recording lapse during a production run
rows.imp_rat: Reconstructing evidence after a recording lapse is burdensome and audit-sensitive, but it is a recovery task rather than a primary control function.
rows.sat_rat: Redundant logging minimizes lapses, but when they occur reconstruction from secondary records is manual and imperfect.

rows.source_job: feel in control of zone conditions
rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of uncertainty about whether zone conditions are truly holding steady within limits at the present moment
rows.imp_rat: Real-time confidence that zones are truly holding steady underpins the technician's felt sense of control over the classified environment.
rows.sat_rat: Live dashboards provide near-real-time assurance, but sensor lag and trust-in-instrument questions leave some present-moment uncertainty.

rows.source_job: feel in control of zone conditions
rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of unexpected condition drifts that undermine the operator's confidence during a shift
rows.imp_rat: Unexpected drifts during a shift erode the technician's confidence and force reactive scrambling rather than steady oversight.
rows.sat_rat: Stable modern control makes surprises less frequent, but unanticipated drifts still occur during load and external changes.

rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of compliance lapses on the operator's watch that erode their standing as a trusted guardian of zone integrity
rows.imp_rat: Compliance lapses on their watch directly damage the technician's standing as the trusted guardian of zone integrity.
rows.sat_rat: Reliable control keeps lapses infrequent, so the technician's guardian standing is usually intact but not guaranteed under upset.

rows.stmt: Reduce the likelihood of pressure or condition failures under demanding operations that call the operator's reliability into question
rows.imp_rat: Pressure or condition failures during demanding operations publicly call the technician's reliability into question with those depending on them.
rows.sat_rat: Robust cascade control handles most demanding periods, but simultaneous stressors can still produce reputation-denting failures.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree to which the operator cannot demonstrate continuous compliance to those who rely on their guardianship
rows.imp_rat: Being able to demonstrate continuous compliance to auditors and QA is central to how the technician's guardianship is recognized.
rows.sat_rat: Historian records generally let them show continuous compliance, though data gaps and divergence occasionally weaken that demonstration.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC Design Engineer (A&E / Process Architect)
rows.stmt: Minimize the amount of oversized air-handling capacity specified beyond the conditioning demand of each zone
rows.imp_rat: Right-sizing capacity matters for cost and energy, but this engineer's job tolerates conservative oversizing, so it is a secondary concern versus performance risk.
rows.sat_rat: Load-calculation tools and margin conventions let engineers size reasonably, though deliberate over-specification remains the accepted status-quo hedge.

rows.stk: Cleanroom HVAC Design Engineer (A&E / Process Architect)
rows.source_job: Authority on Compliant Zone Air Design
rows.stmt: Minimize the frequency of having specifications overruled or reworked by other authorities, eroding recognized authority
rows.imp_rat: Having specifications overruled or reworked erodes the recognized authority this engineer seeks, but it is a status concern secondary to preventing contamination failures.
rows.sat_rat: Strong standards backing generally protects the designer's specs from being overturned, though multi-authority reviews still generate rework.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of repeat maintenance interventions on airflow conditioning issues that recur after being addressed
rows.imp_rat: Recurring repeat interventions signal unresolved root causes and waste maintenance capacity the manager plans.
rows.sat_rat: Root-cause discipline varies; some issues recur because fixes address symptoms, keeping satisfaction moderate.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent executing verification testing of conditioning parameters against defined criteria
rows.imp_rat: Reducing verification test execution time improves throughput but does not change whether the zone meets acceptance criteria.
rows.sat_rat: Modern instrumentation and data loggers speed collection, but recovery tests and smoke studies remain inherently time-consuming.

rows.stmt: Reduce the amount of staff and material resources allocated to airflow conditioning that do not contribute to keeping it within target
rows.imp_rat: Trimming non-contributing staff and material effort improves efficiency but ranks below assurance objectives for this job.
rows.sat_rat: Without clear reliability-impact analytics, resources are often allocated by tradition, leaving low-value effort embedded in current practice.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent reconstructing operating evidence after the production cycle has ended
rows.imp_rat: Reconstructing evidence after the cycle is an administrative burden rather than a live conditioning risk for this operator.
rows.sat_rat: Automated historians retain most data, so post-cycle reconstruction is generally manageable under current systems.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of retuning cycles required to achieve a correct sequence response to changing zone demand
rows.imp_rat: Reducing retuning cycles saves the technician effort, but it is an efficiency concern rather than a direct zone-integrity risk.
rows.sat_rat: Experience and auto-tune features reduce iterations, though several cycles are still often needed for changing demand profiles.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of lingering doubt about whether an excursion has been fully resolved after recovery
rows.imp_rat: Lingering doubt about full resolution adds stress but does not itself alter the technician's control execution once the data reads compliant.
rows.sat_rat: Trend confirmation gives some reassurance, but the absence of guaranteed root-cause closure leaves residual doubt after recoveries.

rows.stmt: Reduce the number of resubmissions required to gain capital approval for the capability acquisition
rows.imp_rat: Approval resubmissions cost time and standing but are a procedural friction rather than a core supply-risk outcome.
rows.sat_rat: Governance templates make first-pass approval achievable, though revisions still occur on incomplete cases.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of energy-use fluctuation that does not correspond to changes in conditioning demand
rows.imp_rat: Demand-uncorrelated energy fluctuation signals inefficiency but has limited direct bearing on classified-zone compliance.
rows.sat_rat: Fixed-speed and coarse control schemes cause energy swings unrelated to load, only partially mitigated where VFDs exist.
